free_text: Reaction mechanisms with loosely bound nuclei
7Li + 6Li at forward angles in the incident energy
University of Kalyani, Kalyani, West Bengal-
The target was made from enriched 6Li material and
was kept thick enough to stop the beam in the target
detectors were placed at forward angles (0., 3., 5.,
10., 20., and 30.) on either side of the beam.
Monitor detectors were placed at +-12.5. for beam
Energy calibration of the acquired spectra were carried
out using proton recoil from the target and cutoff
Inclusive data were acquired with standard NIM
electronics and processed in CAMAC-based data
Extensive theoretical analysis of the continuum
spectrum in the framework of the statistical model
(for compound nuclear emissions) and Serber model for
the breakup process was carried out. In addition to
alpha and proton spectra the triton spectrum was also
analyzed as it is the breakup partner of alpha from 7Li
data were digitized from Figs 2,3,4,5 and 6
No any answer from corresponding author
